prompts.seed:
Classify the given text into one of these categories: alarm, apr, balance, book_flight, book_hotel, calculator, calories, car_rental, cook_time, credit_limit, credit_score, definition, direct_deposit, freeze_account, lost_luggage, meeting_schedule, pay_bill, payday, pto_request, recipe, report_fraud, report_lost_card, restaurant_reservation, restaurant_reviews, rewards_balance, taxes, timer, transfer, travel_suggestion, weather.

Respond with exactly one category name from the list above and nothing else.
prompts.x1:
Classify the given text into one of these categories: alarm, apr, balance, book_flight, book_hotel, calculator, calories, car_rental, cook_time, credit_limit, credit_score, definition, direct_deposit, freeze_account, lost_luggage, meeting_schedule, pay_bill, payday, pto_request, recipe, report_fraud, report_lost_card, restaurant_reservation, restaurant_reviews, rewards_balance, taxes, timer, transfer, travel_suggestion, weather.

Key distinctions:
- pto_request: requesting time off from work (vacation requests, PTO, leave)
- travel_suggestion: asking for travel recommendations or ideas (where to go)
- payday: asking when salary/paycheck arrives or is scheduled
- direct_deposit: setting up or managing automatic deposit of paychecks (employer paying employees)
- book_hotel: booking or finding hotels to stay at (focus on accommodation)
- restaurant_reviews: reading or asking about restaurant opinions/ratings
- pay_bill: paying bills or expenses owed by the user

Decision rules:
- If text is about requesting vacation time off work, classify as pto_request (not travel_suggestion)
- If text mentions when salary arrives or payment schedule, classify as payday (not direct_deposit)
- If text is about employer paying employees or payroll setup, classify as direct_deposit (not pay_bill or payday)
- If text is about finding/booking hotels even with mention of reviews, classify as book_hotel (not restaurant_reviews)

Respond with exactly one category name from the list above and nothing else.
prompts.x2:
Classify the given text into one of these categories: alarm, apr, balance, book_flight, book_hotel, calculator, calories, car_rental, cook_time, credit_limit, credit_score, definition, direct_deposit, freeze_account, lost_luggage, meeting_schedule, pay_bill, payday, pto_request, recipe, report_fraud, report_lost_card, restaurant_reservation, restaurant_reviews, rewards_balance, taxes, timer, transfer, travel_suggestion, weather.

Key distinctions:
- pto_request: requesting time off from work (vacation requests, PTO, leave)
- travel_suggestion: asking for travel recommendations or ideas (where to go)
- payday: asking when salary/paycheck arrives or is scheduled
- direct_deposit: setting up or managing automatic deposit of paychecks (employer paying employees)
- book_hotel: booking or finding hotels to stay at (focus on accommodation)
- restaurant_reviews: reading or asking about restaurant opinions/ratings
- pay_bill: paying bills or expenses owed by the user
- lost_luggage: reporting missing, lost, or delayed baggage/luggage during travel
- report_lost_card: reporting a lost, stolen, or missing credit/debit card

Decision rules:
- If text is about requesting vacation time off work, classify as pto_request (not travel_suggestion)
- If text mentions when salary arrives or payment schedule, classify as payday (not direct_deposit)
- If text is about employer paying employees or payroll setup, classify as direct_deposit (not pay_bill or payday)
- If text is about finding/booking hotels even with mention of reviews, classify as book_hotel (not restaurant_reviews)
- If text mentions luggage, baggage, or suitcases being lost/missing during travel, classify as lost_luggage (not report_lost_card)
- If text mentions a card (credit card, debit card, bank card) being lost or stolen, classify as report_lost_card (not lost_luggage)

Respond with exactly one category name from the list above and nothing else.
